entry.title: Advice on Curriculum Basis Date for clients new global SF-LMS instance
entry.content:
Hi there,

I have a question in regards to the Curriculum Basis Date in Global Application Settings. The client i am working for already have SF-LMS Module. In their existing BAU LMS module, which they"ve had for a number of years, they have configured the Curriculum Basis Date to 01/Jan/2010. 

Since the Client is a global company with offices in many geographies, they are in the process of rolling out a brand new SF system (incl LMS) to their global workforce. As part of configuring the system and setting it up, I have a question in relationt to whether the Curriculum Basis Date (Global Application Settings) in the to-be SF-LMS instance should retain the original curriculum date from their current BAU LMS instance (i.e. 01/Jan/2010) or should it be a new date. I ask this question because we will be migrated Learning History records to the new SF-LMS system. Therefore my thinking is that any Curriculas that need to be migrated to the new LMS as well as the learning history records (relating to Learners learning history being migrated from AS-IS LMS) should be based on the same Curriculum date that is maintained in SF-BizX (01/Jan/2010), as opposed to configuring a new date. Please advise

entry.title: Is there an option to view which projects have been modified most recently using a filter
entry.content:
Is there a way to view directly in the project management module which projects have been modified the last time? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there an option to view which projects have been m...
entry.content: The advanced filter for projects does not include the "Changed On" date. As a result, you are unable to see which projects have been most recently modified.

entry.title: Why is final confirmation, no remaining work, and clear open res. automatically checked off in IW41
entry.content:
When entering in time confirmations in IW41, the final confirmation, no remaining work and clear open res. are automatically checked off.

How do I update the system so when I go into IW41 these boxes are not checked off?

entry.title: Re: Add an accounting document to an SD sales flow
entry.content: Thank you, you helped me with many questions! So if I want to add an accounting document after the billing processing, it should be something Z.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit : Migrate your Data using Staging Tables a CO-Cost Center Object c
entry.content: @Caio Thank you for enriching the article, your contribution is very valuable.

entry.title: Re: Memo Line rules in SAP ByD
entry.content:
Hi ,

Basically there is no option to maintain memo line rules for bank accounts. A particular company has a bank account , but the bank account should not have a company.

Try to check this documentation guide for creation of memo line rule: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/0635ec3491974ad988be05d6b1dcf734/2cb9b071722d1014bf6fcf9dad622515.html?locale=en-US

Best Regards,

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: Cannot open SAPUI5 Fiori Applications after set openM...
entry.content:
You have to be specific, "doesn't work" means what?

I think you need to use embedIntoDetailsNestedRouter if you want to load your detail page to the inbox

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements
entry.content:
We are thinking of implementing SAP and are therefore looking for a solution for our Polish subsidiary to cover the requirements of the Polish tax authorities for mandatory SAF-T reporting. As we plan to use SAP S/4HANA public cloud, the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements cannot be used (as far as I understood).

In 2023 ‘SAP Document and Reporting Compliance’ was not yet able to cover the requirements of the Polish authorities. But what about today?

In this webinar (Standard Audit File-Tax (SAF-T) - Statutory Reporting in Poland (JPK) - SAP Product Strategy) here at the end of the last year there was an item on the agenda called "'Transition from SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements’ to ‘SAP Document and Reporting Compliance’".

entry.title: Re: SAP Power Designer Excel Bulk Upload on Entity > Mapp...
entry.content:
Hi Vipul,

I am afraid, that you are asking too much (from PD Excel import). I`ve tried several times to import mappings using standard Excel Import extension and my each conclusion was that it cannot be done.

As a result, I created my own automated mappings import from Excel. Pros and cons are obvious. You can tailor the script for importing mappings to your current Excel structure. However, you still have to take into account mandatory items such as Data Sources, Attribute mapping cannot exist without a parent Entity mapping, etc. Disadvantage is also clear. You have to spend time by writing the import script. And believe me, importing mappings is not a matter of 200 lines of VB script. There are surprisingly many things/rules you have check to make sure, your imported data are consistent and PD-valid.

Unlike me, you have the great advantage of being able to use my previously acquired knowledge and save time doing your own import. Let me know on LinkedIn if you are interested.

Have a nice day,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content: Thank you @hariprasadgud , I raised 77464/2025 for it.

entry.title: Re: Disenchantment: GPT for generating ABAP code documentation
entry.content:
I think you are an expert trying to see if ChatGPT would do a better job than you could, at a highly specialized task. I don't think that's what AI is currently aiming at, at all.

I use AI in my personal life regularly to help me write emails for example to my children's doctors and teachers, especially because I'm not a very good writer in German, and it's amazing. Now, would a professional translator do a better job at this task? Very probably. And maybe there are professional translators doing the same exercise you are doing, sharing with other translators that AI is still doing an inferior job to what they would do, but they are missing the point, aren't they? The point is that, in many situations, people don't need perfect. People don't need the best. Many times, people just need "good enough". And this will definitely lower the workload for many professionals around the world.

entry.title: Re: Intent-based Navigation - semanticObjectAction annota...
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_winkler ,

Same results. Still the popover action list comes up, instead of navigating directly to the semantic action defined in the annotation.

Seems like it correctly interprets the annotation for semanticObject but totally ignores the Action

semanticObjectAction: 'displayDocument',

entry.title: Uploading simulations and a book page with long text and subsequently converting it into a hands-on
entry.content:
I would like to ask for your help.
I need to create a methodology for the S4/HANA application. It will consist of screenshots (simulations), but before these screenshots, there must be 3 pages of written text (introduction to the application, description of the fields found in the application). I tried inserting a book page into the simulation, but the text is too long and doesn't fit into a single book page.

I need to then convert the entire simulation into a Word format and have it translated into English using a translation machine. Is there any way to insert long text into the simulation and make it scrollable? Can users scroll through it later in the library?

Thank you so much for your reply, and have a nice day.

Helena

entry.title: Re: External Event - required fields
entry.content: Hi - we leverage the external event form where they submit their course completion and manager has to review so they can approve or deny. I can not be adding over 100 titles in SF so it ties to an item ID. Users are taking different trainings outside SF/Percipio systems.

entry.title: Re: Total Target Training Hours Requirement
entry.content: Hi there- thanks for your comment. Unfortunately creating item IDs in SF is not ideal since its not the same trainings employees are taking. It's all different job functions. We currently have SF and Percipio and we have the percipio connector feeding completions to SF. Users fill out the external event request form that is route to manager for approval or denial so the email template to me would not be suitable.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Available - to - Promise (aATP in S/4HANA) version 2023
entry.content:
Hello Mithun,

Check if the Fiori App Supports Transport Requests

Certain Fiori apps provide an option to save configurations into a transport request.
Navigate to the Fiori app you are using for aATP configurations (e.g., Manage ATP Rules, Manage Backorder Processing, etc.).
Look for options like "Save to Transport" or "Assign Transport Request" when saving configurations.
If available, assign the configuration changes to a transport request.

Regrads,

Ambarish

entry.title: Re: External Event - required fields
entry.content: thank you

entry.title: Can SAP Migrate date be used to modify or upload data in SAP Build App?
Re: Audience Validation Failed Persists Document Information Extraction
entry.content: Audience Validation Failed - means when your token was decoded, it couldn't verify the "aud" from it. So make sure to check your token properly. use a jwt.io online service to verify your token. All should be good. I had the same issue, this was the case.

entry.title: how to manage user authentication in sap build code?
entry.content:
I am working on SAP Build Code and need some clarification regarding user authentication and role-based access control. Specifically, I have the following two questions:

  1. Is it possible to restrict access to specific columns in a table based on user roles? For example, if I have a "salaries" column, can I make it so that only users with a "Manager" role can see this column, while users with a "Viewer" role cannot?

  2. Regarding action buttons, if a user has a "Read Only" role, I understand that they will receive a "Forbidden" message when attempting actions like delete or create. Is there a way to dynamically hide these action buttons (e.g., "Delete" or "Create") for users with "Read Only" access, so they don't even appear on the UI?

Looking forward to your insights.

entry.title: How can i create role with object or action and manage authorization for sap build app?
Re: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications
entry.content:
Dear @SteffenKoette ,

I have checked again and it is working correctly today without making any changes.
I received emails on 16/01 saying that SAC was having problems, is it possible that it was a temporary bug due to the problems on that day?
The important thing is that the filters are now working correctly, even with the tables in present mode.
Thank you very much for your support.

Marsida

entry.title: Re: Disenchantment: GPT for generating ABAP code documentation
entry.content: @bashope I agree. Here, the text generated by GenAI was completely useless: it's impossible to understand what the program exactly does by reading it. So, here "perfect" or "good enough" is not the problem. GenAI just can't do this type of job, to retro-document a complex program (NB: one of the worst-written program I could see in my (long) career).

entry.title: SAP Best practices in Public cloud/Private cloud
entry.content:
Please help me If you've any idea on below request

I want to know SAP standard functions availability in private and public editions.

I can see that the SAP portal shows more best practices for public cloud than for private cloud.

Is this because of less customization available on Public Edition, so SAP allows more standard functions to use?

 

Example: Intercompany Sales Order Processing - Make-to-Order(6GC) standard function available on Public cloud but not listed in Private edition.

 

Reference portal:Process Navigator - SAP for Me

Thank you!

BR,

Christina

entry.title: Re: SAC - Translation of public Dimensions
entry.content: Meta data transaltion is only for property name(column header), not for property value

entry.title: Mastering Custom Integrations in SAP Datasphere: Leveraging Open Connectors for REST APIs
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Datasphere offers a wide range of pre-defined source system connectors. However, there may be instances where a standard connector is unavailable, such as with certain REST APIs. To address this, SAP provides the option to leverage Open Connectors. This blog outlines the process, including:

  • Setting up the connection to SAP Open Connectors
  • Creating a custom connector
  • Integrating the data into SAP Datasphere

Setup BTP and Datasphere Connection

The initial step in setting up SAP Open Connectors in SAP Datasphere is to assign the Integration Suite entity to your BTP subaccount.


Once the assignment is completed, you can launch the Integration Suite from the subaccount.


It is crucial to ensure that the user has been granted the "Integration_Provisioner" role to enable the necessary access rights.


Within the Integration Suite, select "Open Connectors" as the required capability.


We can now open the Open Connectors


Now the Open Connectors – Integration Suite is opened.


Next, to integrate the Open Connectors account within SAP Datasphere, navigate to the "Connections" tab in the desired space and select "Integrate your SAP Open Connectors Account."


You will be prompted to enter specific parameters.

The organization Open Connectors secrets you can get when click on the settings button in the Open Connectors.


Once the parameters are correctly configured, the account connection will be established successfully.

This process is also described in the SAP Documentation.

Build Custom Connector

SAP Open Connectors provides an extensive catalog of predefined connectors for various APIs and services (Documentation). In this example, we use a public API that provides Harry Potter-related data (Documentation). To create the custom connector, go to the "Connectors" section and click "Build New Connector."


You can either create a connector from scratch or import an existing configuration.


First, provide the connector's name, description, and classification. In the "Setup" step, enter the Base URL of the API and configure other relevant details.


Since the chosen API does not require authentication, remove the default authentication headers using a PreRequestHook.


In the "Resources" section, define the API endpoints to be consumed by mapping the endpoint URL to a custom endpoint name (in this case, they are identical). Additionally, configure the HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.) and any optional parameters.


It is essential to define the expected response model.

 


The response model can also be defined based on a sample payload.


After saving the configuration, validate the connector in the "Validation" section.


After saving the configuration, validate the connector in the "Validation" section. The validation process will highlight any errors or missing definitions. Once the connector passes validation, authenticate an instance of the connector to begin testing.



Now you can “Authenticate” an instance of your connector to test it.

 


In the "API Docs" section, select the instance and test the endpoints to review the response codes and payloads.



The result of the test shows us the response code and body:

 


The "Activity" menu provides basic logging and monitoring, which is invaluable for troubleshooting issues during testing.



In the next section we describe now how we add this connector as a Source System and consume it.

 

Consumption of Connector in SAP Datasphere

After connecting the Open Connectors account to your Datasphere space, create a new connection by selecting "Open Connector."


Choose the instance of your custom connector, noting that only Data Flows are currently supported for data integration.


With the connection established, create a new Data Flow in the Data Builder. The custom connector and its defined endpoints will now appear as selectable sources.


By configuring the data flow and running it successfully, the REST API data is written into a local table in SAP Datasphere, making it available for use in reporting and analysis.



This process demonstrates how SAP Datasphere, combined with Open Connectors, can integrate external REST API data into your enterprise data landscape, providing enriched data insights and seamless connectivity for custom use cases.

 

Conclusion

In summary, integrating REST APIs into SAP Datasphere using Open Connectors provides a robust framework for expanding data connectivity beyond standard sources, enabling seamless integration of external data into your SAP landscape. This process involves configuring the BTP environment, building custom connectors, and defining API endpoints for efficient data flow into SAP Datasphere tables. While the integration offers extensive flexibility, it is crucial to be aware of its limitations, such as support constraints and the requirement for data flow-based consumption (Documentation). By leveraging Open Connectors' logging and validation tools, developers can ensure smooth implementation and quickly address potential errors. This capability enhances data-driven decision-making by combining internal datasets with external API sources, enriching reports and analytics through SAP Analytics Cloud for more comprehensive insights.

Links

Open Connectors: https://help.openconnectors.ext.hana.ondemand.com/home

Open Connectors Catalog: https://help.openconnectors.ext.hana.ondemand.com/home/catalog

Building Custom Connectors: https://help.openconnectors.ext.hana.ondemand.com/home/build-elements

Open Connectors Datasphere: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/9bfe7db51216449d985a0b59f5e181c4.html

entry.title: Re: SAP Pint: A Beginner-Friendly Chrome Extension Tool for Integration Developers
entry.content:
I added the base 64 encoding and decoding. Please check the latest with the new extension. 

 

Key Features
Format Tools
The updated description now includes:
Core format & conversion tools:
JSON/XML/CSV/YAML conversions
Text formatting and case conversion
Base64 encoding/decoding
Time tools:
Current time in multiple formats
ISO/UTC time conversions
Unix timestamp
XML tools:
XPath evaluation
XML Schema validation
XML to XSD generation
Developer features:
SAP integration resources
Character count
Copy functionality
Resizable interface

entry.title: Variant update problem with variant management control ( sap.ui.fl.variants )
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
I haven an issue with VariantManagement from sap.ui.fl library where it creates variant without any problem on `save` event. After it is saved, if i try to update it by adding new filters on screen but it fails to update variant. There is no error or anything on screen. When i check network tab, i can see request during initial save for creation but not with update. Overrite attribute is set to true and same codes are triggered in save. 
sample:

  variantManagerXML.attachEvent("save", (event) => {
                    try {
                        const filterData = mainFilter.getData();
                        const mParameters = event.getParameters();
                        mParameters.contexts = {
                            content: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(filterData))
                        };
                        this.byId("vm").setModified(true);
                    } catch (error) {
                        console.error("Error during save:", error);
                    }
                });

 Where can be the problem on update ?

entry.title: Re: Uploading simulations and a book page with long text...
entry.content:
In the workarea tree, each simulation has a Description field. This is for text, pictures, and the occasional video, but it doesn't have all the bells and whistles of a Book Page. The Description field should do what you need to do - have a lot of text. Paste your text into there as Plain Text, format it the way you want, and then when you generate your Word document from the simulation (such as a Standard document), make sure that your Tools > Settings > Documentation Settings > [document format] > Fragment section includes the Description (which Standard format does by default). Generate the document, and your pages and pages of text should display before the screenshots in your simulation. 

You can then send that Word doc out for translation. I always use the Producer's Automatic Translation function, which will spit out the Description text in either Word or XLIFF for your translator service to process. You can also use the SAP Translation Hub, which is included in your Cloud SEN subscription.

You can embed the same text in a Book Page that is linked into your sim, as you noted, and the text box is scrollable by default unless you turn off the scroll function for it. Users may not notice the scroll bar, so I would call their attention to it at the top of the text in the text box. 

You can also use the Scrollable Book Page. There is a template here SAP Enable Now Info Center . I don't know which would be more obvious to use for your users. 

Shane or other smart people, please correct me as needed.

entry.title: Re: Reverse a Material to dekit the components
entry.content: Dominik, Thank you so what you're stating is that the production order would need to be referenced in the MIGO. My ask is that is there a tcode where someone could enter a Material A and it would create the lines to be dekit and returned to inventory? Our old ERP system MS Dynamics had a function call a BOM Journal where you could enter the material number and based on the qty would create the component lines that you could enter batch number and location to dekit it.

entry.title: Re: Can we configure multiple sender systems to fetch dat...
entry.content:
Hi @SinhaSouvik ,

In local integration process, start event would not allow directly to connect with an adapter. You can instead use request reply step for this

entry.title: Re: Sending files from different folders/directories at a...
entry.content:
Hello Bhargav,

There is no standard way to poll files different from different folders.

You can achieve this in below ways : 

1. You can use 3 local integration processes and configure 3 different SFTP adapters using request reply  and pull the required files. In main integration flow you can handle the payloads as per your requirement.

Thank you in advance.

When the user click the button, the form will not open.

 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Why is final confirmation, no remaining work, and cle...

Use Tcode - OIOR,

Accroding your requirenment select plant and Order type,

Untick default Values.

entry.title: Using Field Tab in matchcode F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST
entry.content:
Hi all, I have a problem using the F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST function.
What happens is that when I run the routine that consumes the F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST function, it shows me the data I need, but with incorrect header titles, for example: F0001, F0002, F0003. For this reason, I am creating a fieldtab with the titles of the headers that I want to show, but when I run the program with the fieldtab, it returns the MANDT data repeated in all the fields.
Could you help me by telling me if I am missing something? Thank you very much!

 

TYPES:
    BEGIN OF ty_module,
      Mandt       TYPE Mandt,
      Row	      TYPE INT4,
      Process     TYPE ZBI_BEN_S01H-PROCESS,
    END OF ty_module.
	
DATA: it_module       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_module,
it_ret_values   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval.

FORM SHOW_F4_MODULE.

  DATA: wa_fcat   TYPE DFIES,
        it_fcmod  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF DFIES.

  REFRESH: it_ret_values.

  IF it_module IS INITIAL.
    ObjBenMto->GetModule(
      IMPORTING
        rtdat = it_module ).
  ENDIF.

  CLEAR wa_fcat.
  wa_fcat-tabname   = 'IT_MODULE'.
  wa_fcat-fieldname = 'MANDT'.
  wa_fcat-intlen    = 5.
  wa_fcat-outputlen = 10.
  APPEND wa_fcat TO it_fcmod.

  CLEAR wa_fcat.
  wa_fcat-tabname   = 'IT_MODULE'.
  wa_fcat-fieldname = 'ROW'.
  wa_fcat-intlen    = 5.
  wa_fcat-outputlen = 10.
  APPEND wa_fcat TO it_fcmod.

  CLEAR wa_fcat.
  wa_fcat-tabname   = 'IT_MODULE'.
  wa_fcat-fieldname = 'PROCESS'.
  wa_fcat-intlen    = 50.
  wa_fcat-outputlen = 50.
  APPEND wa_fcat TO it_fcmod.

  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    
    CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
      EXPORTING
        retfield        = 'PROCESS'
        window_title    = 'Process'
        value_org       = 'S'
      TABLES
        value_tab       = it_module
        field_tab       = it_fcmod
        return_tab      = it_ret_values
      EXCEPTIONS
        parameter_error = 1
        no_values_found = 2
        OTHERS          = 3.

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      READ TABLE it_ret_values ASSIGNING <fs_rval> INDEX 1.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        p_mod   = <fs_rval>-fieldval.
        gv_mod  = <fs_rval>-fieldval.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.

 

the table I get at the end is similar to this one:

MANDTROWPROCESS
200200200
200200200

entry.title: Re: Why is final confirmation, no remaining work, and cle...
entry.content: Thank you very much

entry.title: Deactivation of Compliance Requirements in SAP S/4HANA Product Compliance 2408
entry.content:
Hi product compliance team, hi guys,
today I am looking working in Substance Volume Tracking [SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408], however; the topics belongs to the handling of the compliance requirement versions.

The help.sap.com states: "Normally, when a new version is activated, the older version is set to Inactive."

Better and more precise would be a text as "normally, when a new version gets activated, the system sets the predecessor version to inactive" 🙂

 

Starting point: the Apr.2021 is historical but still active. This is a valid use cases.

I am on the way to activate the latest available version...

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

I activate it.

add legal area for composition

System states that the activation takes place and that the previous requirement is deactivated...

So far, so good...

However; for me ist seems that it is still active...

 

 

The application log does not state anything...

So I was required to deactivate it manually...

 

 

 

 

Did I do something wrong or why the "old" compliance requirement version was not deactivated?
Thanks
Harry

entry.title: org.xml.sax.SAXParseException: Content is not allowed in prolog Error
entry.content:
Greetings,

 I am looking at the sap bi 4.3 tomcat and stdout.log file and see continuously the following error. How I can find out which XML file is causing this error?

Caused by: org.opensaml.xml.parse.XMLParserException: Invalid XML
at org.opensaml.xml.parse.StaticBasicParserPool.parse(StaticBasicParserPool.java:237) ~[xmltooling-1.4.4.jar:?]
at org.opensaml.saml2.metadata.provider.AbstractMetadataProvider.unmarshallMetadata(AbstractMetadataProvider.java:460) ~[opensaml-2.6.6.jar:?]
at org.opensaml.saml2.metadata.provider.AbstractReloadingMetadataProvider.unmarshallMetadata(AbstractReloadingMetadataProvider.java:312) ~[opensaml-2.6.6.jar:?]
... 162 more
Caused by: org.xml.sax.SAXParseException: Content is not allowed in prolog.
at com.sun.org.apache.xerces.internal.util.ErrorHandlerWrapper.createSAXParseException(ErrorHandlerWrapper.java:204) ~[?:1.8.0_381]

....

entry.title: Re: CF Deployment Error: Error getting tenant t0
entry.content: From my end, it was resolved through the config inside the package.json under cds.requires.

entry.title: DSO calculation logic.
entry.content:
DSO is not showing the incorrect values.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: DSO calculation logic.
entry.content:
Please, find the solution given in SAP KBA LINK: - https://me.sap.com/notes/2919427/E

Refer the help portal link:- 

"How the Key Figures in Credit Management Are Calculated" 

"How the DSO Is Calculated"

DSO is calculated as follows:- 

TB = Total Balance = Balance carried forward + Total of the Debit posting - Total of the credit posting 

SOS = Sum of Sales = Sales during last 3 posting periods  plus the sales in currently used period.

Days = Number of days in the last three posting periods  plus the currently used period.

Sales per day (S/D)= SOS/Days 

DSO = TB / (S/D).

 

(The DSO calculated from the data in KNC1 table).

entry.title: Re: Can't update KF in Planning Level but can in MDW
entry.content:
Hi,

PLOBs maintenance in MDW is only showing stored keyf, not the calculated one. I think your change with currency made you changing the semantic of your keyf

 

Cheers, Daniel

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Client & SAP GUI: Not launching GUI for Windows
entry.content:
We finally found the solution:

The SAP S/4HANA server's (we use embedded deployment for Fiori apps) domain, corresponding to the base URI of the FLP, had to be registered as "trusted site" in the client machine's internet settings.

On your machine, search for Internet Properties -> Security -> Trusted sites -> add system host address

Note that this setting may be managed by your company's system administrator through policy settings.

entry.title: Re: Will there be version 11 of SAP business one
entry.content: It is 2025 and no update to business one. Will SAP still be working on version 10?

entry.title: Re: Increase "page size" or growing threshold in a respon...
entry.content: Thanks for your answer but for standard Apps and since the option is back with the next release I don't think the adaption effort can be approved. I was hoping for some kind of hack, but thanks anyway for your answer

entry.title: Re: Master database creation failed for SAP ASE 16.0 SP03...
entry.content:
Looks like the same problem I ran into a few years back.

While the normal solution is to add traceflag -T11889 to the RUNSERVER file, said solution assumes the dataserver has already been created.

In my case I used the following workaround:

$ cd /opt/sybase/ASE_160_SP03_PL07/ASE-16_0/bin

$ mv dataserver .dataserver

##### create new 'dataserver' file with following contents:

$ cat dataserver
/opt/sybase/ASE_160_SP03_PL07/ASE-16_0/bin/.dataserver -T11889 "$@"

$ chmod 755 dataserver

$ srvbuildres -r your.dataserver.resource.file.rs

Cleanup steps once the dataserver is created:

  • mv .dataserver dataserver
  • add -T11889 to the ASE's RUNSERVER file

NOTES:

  • This was a one-off, quick-n-dirty solution hence the minimal script and hardcoded path.
  • I don't have access to the PAM or KBAs so I don't know if this issue has been addressed in newer ASE and/or linux versions.

entry.title: Printing word document from binary data
entry.content:
Hi,

I am receiving the binary data of a word document from external system with OData.

I have to send this document to printer for automatic printing in back-ground.

Is there any function module or class methods?

Thanks in advance,

Sreeni

entry.title: Re: Sending files from different folders/directories at a...
entry.content:
The only way this is possible is to check the "Include Subdirectories" option of the processing tab, which will work if and only if the three directories are subdirectories of the one specified in your iFlow configuration.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

can we contact you about the Portuguese Plug-in?

Best Regards

Laura

The users should work and not play .

Martin

I am recieving the binary data of word document from external system with Odata.

I have convert this binary data to PDF file in the background and attach to the Business document.

Sreeni

entry.title: Re: Centralize Authorization Management of SAP Cloud ALM Using the Identity Provisioning Service
entry.content:
Your set-up probably entails granular access rights. You want to check with the admin if you can have 'Configure Provisioning Authorizations'  and then "MANAGE_PROVISIONING_SOURCES"

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/beta-configure-authorizations-based-on-policies?

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now Learning Journey
entry.content:
Hi @egcarden 

It looks like this is a new question. 
I would like to encourage to always use our Ask Questions section and post your question there.
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses.
Would you mind to repost it ?
Please also include the link to the SAP Enable Now Learning Journey you are referring to.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Webinars 2025 - Portuguese and Spanish
entry.content:
@bashope Olá Bruno! Obrigada pela sua pergunta, então, por agora em um primeiro momento de 2025 sim, serão apenas de operation. Mas no fim de 2024 fizemos sessões de Implementation e temos as gravações disponíveis em https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-alm-webinars-2024-portuguese/ba-p/13980614 , espero que te ajude 🙂 

Mantenha-se conectado para ver as próximas divulgações de Webinars! Tenha um bom dia.

entry.title: Re: Wrong conversion of amount to condition value in Purc...
entry.content:
Please refer the note 

2184240 - Rounding of condition values in PO, issues with non-decimal currency (JPY, IDR, etc) - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Optimizing Production Order Process: Leveraging SAP BTP Event Mesh for Decoupling Inbound Scenario
entry.content:
We were working on a scenario for a greenfield implementation project, with the initial phase focused on a newly built plant. The technologies used included SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, Integration Suite on Cloud Foundry, and a third-party Manufacturing Execution System (MES). The scenario revolved around the production order process, encompassing activities like backflush and confirmations.

For confirmations, we employed the Auto GR (Goods Receipt) process. From a technical standpoint, we planned to implement API-based development and integrations, adhering to SAP best practices. Before diving into the technical solution, let me first provide the context of the problem and the reason for the change.

In the initial phase, the design dictated that SAP S/4HANA would expose only the OData APIs required for the production order process, while the MES team would handle the remaining responsibilities. You may already be questioning this approach, and rightfully so—I completely agree. There was indeed a flaw in the design process, but by the time it was identified, it was too late to implement major changes. We had no choice but to proceed with the existing design, while remaining open to revisiting it in the future.

Problem Statement: We implemented the design as planned, where the MES system utilized Azure technologies to make calls and process confirmations and backflushes. Initially, everything functioned well due to the low volume of data. However, as the shop floor operations scaled and multiple systems began simultaneously triggering GR (Goods Receipt) and backflush requests, the system started throwing errors.

The issue stemmed from API-level locking inherent in SAP’s design, where only one request could process a production order at a time. If a request was already being processed, subsequent ones failed due to the lock. Additionally, the second flaw was that the MES team could only see the default error response code provided by the OData API. This lack of visibility left the S/4HANA team unable to provide effective support or handle exceptions.

The SAP S/4HANA team could not provide support or implement exception handling due to the absence of an application layer or any development and integration mechanisms to monitor errors effectively. After analyzing the issue, we requested the MES team to implement a 5-second delay for processing confirmations and backflushes. While this solution initially worked for a few orders (around 10), it quickly failed under higher data volumes, as the same error reoccurred.

To address this recurring issue, we brainstormed alternative approaches and proposed introducing Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) into EDA into the landscape. This approach involved leveraging SAP Event Mesh on SAP BTP and building an integration layer using SAP Integration Suite to process the data efficiently. The resulting high-level architecture offered a scalable and resilient solution to overcome these challenges.

 

Let me explain the steps we followed to implement the solution.

Prerequisites

Before proceeding, ensure that SAP Event Mesh (EM) is activated in the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) with the necessary roles and authorizations. Once the prerequisites were met, we completed the following configurations.

It is important to note that when activating EM, you must create a service key specific to the Event Mesh. To receive data, you need to share the service key credentials for EM. A common mistake I have observed is sharing the Cloud Integration service keys instead of the Event Mesh service key. These two are not the same, and the service key in SAP BTP serves a specific purpose.

Steps to Configure Event Mesh

  1. Create a Queue in Event Mesh:
    • The queue serves as a receiver for the data to be processed by the system.
  2. Bind the CPI Endpoint to the Queue:
    • Configure the integration flow endpoint in the SAP Cloud Integration Suite as a webhook to the queue. This ensures that incoming messages from the queue are routed to the integration flow for processing into SAP S/4HANA.
  3. Create a Dead Queue:
    • Typically, dead queues are used to handle exception messages. However, in this scenario, we repurposed the dead queue to log and reprocess lock errors. I’ll explain this usage further.
  4. Configure the Integration Flow:
    • Initially, we considered creating a custom integration flow to handle this, but we found pre-packaged integration content that streamlined the implementation. The package is available in the SAP Integration Suite Discover section under the name “Replay Messages from Queues via SAP Event Mesh”.

Using the Pre-Packaged Integration Content

The package contains four artifacts, each with a specific purpose. However, we utilized only three artifacts as there was no need to leverage value mapping which was the fourth artifact.

  1. Retrieving Messages from SAP Event Mesh to JMS:
    • This integration flow retrieves messages from SAP Event Mesh and sends them to the JMS queue.
  2. JMS Router for SAP Event Mesh Messages:
    • The core integration flow routes messages from the JMS queue based on their type or purpose.
  3. Processing Messages:
    • This flow processes the JMS messages retrieved from SAP Event Mesh and sends them to SAP S/4HANA.

By utilizing this pre-packaged content, we eliminated the need to develop a custom solution from scratch, saving significant time and effort. The setup allowed us to handle and process messages efficiently while addressing exceptions such as lock errors through the configured dead queue.

Exposing an Event Mesh (EM) Endpoint to Receive Data

Once a queue is created in SAP Event Mesh, you need to use a prefix to publish or consume the data. Instead of duplicating existing content, I recommend referring to this Exposing EM Endpoint that provides detailed steps for exposing an Event Mesh endpoint.

What We Achieved Compared to the Initial Problem

After implementing the Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) and exposing the Event Mesh endpoint, we addressed several challenges outlined in the initial problem statement. Here’s what we achieved:

  1. Added a Technology Layer to Monitor the Process:
    • By introducing SAP Integration Suite and Event Mesh, we gained better visibility and control over the transaction flow.
  1. Decoupled the Process Using EDA:
    • The MES system was no longer directly coupled to SAP S/4HANA, reducing dependencies and bottlenecks.
  1. Streamlined Transactions from MES to S/4HANA:
    • Transactions flowed seamlessly from the MES system into SAP S/4HANA, thanks to the event-driven approach.
  1. Eliminated MES Exception Handling for Lock Issues:
    • The MES system no longer handled API lock issues, as the Event Mesh took over and returned 204 responses (success status).
  1. Enhanced Monitoring in SAP Integration Suite:
    • All S/4HANA transactions received from the MES system could now be monitored and managed effectively in the SAP Integration Suite.
  1. Automatic Processing of Lock Issues with Dead Queue:
    • Using the Dead Queue concept, API lock issues were processed automatically until successful, ensuring consistency.
  1. Integrated Exception Handling for Data Issues:
    • Alerts for data-related issues were integrated, allowing the data team to address problems promptly.

How We Used the Dead Queue to Process Messages

The Dead Queue concept was applied with a unique approach. Here’s how it worked:

  1. Handling API Lock Issues:
    • When multiple transactions were processed simultaneously, any transaction encountering an API lock was written to the Dead Queue.
  2. Custom Integration Flow:
    • A copy of the standard integration artifact was created, designed to read messages directly from the Dead Queue.
    • The custom flow repeatedly processed the messages until they were successfully posted.
  3. Outcome:
    • This approach allowed us to monitor and handle transactions with lock issues effectively.
    • We could tally the total number of messages processed from the MES system to SAP S/4HANA, ensuring all transactions were accounted for.

Solution to COGI Errors and Decoupling GR and Backflush

After rigorous testing, we concluded that the EDA architecture effectively resolved the primary problem. However, we encountered COGI errors due to standard BAPI locks during the final posting to S/4HANA. To address this, we implemented the following solution:

  1. Decoupling GR and Backflush Processes We introduced changes to separate the Goods Receipt (GR) and Backflush processes, reducing contention at the BAPI level.
      • Decouple GR and Backflush Process for a detailed explanation of how we decoupled the processes.

Final Results

With these enhancements, we successfully processed hundreds of orders simultaneously. By leveraging Event-Driven Architecture and decoupling critical processes, we significantly reduced system dependencies and resource contention.

For high-volume, scalable implementations, I strongly recommend using EDA and decoupling processes wherever possible. This approach not only reduces system dependencies but also enhances performance, monitoring, and resilience.

Note: I have not purposefully included the technical details are there are many blogs from our experts detailing how to use the EM. Incase if you have specific questions please reach out to me. 

Regards 

HS

entry.title: Re: Sending files from different folders/directories at a...
entry.content: Hi Ryan, but in case of multiple files with different filenames to be picked from different folders at same time, we cannot use include sub directories option. Include sub directories only helps to select this option allows you to look for files in all subdirectories of the directory. In CPI we do not have advance source file selection as of SAP PI

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to post to multiple accounting principles when revaluate an asset with ABAW?
entry.content:
Hello, 
If the system is S/4Hana check note 2270388

Revaluation

A revaluation is always posted accounting principle-specific. The Accounting Principle field in transaction ABAWL (Revaluation) is therefore a required entry field.

It is not possible to have manual revaluation postings together with automatic revaluation through indexes on the same fixed asset in the same depreciation area.

entry.title: Re: Add an accounting document to an SD sales flow
entry.content:
If you want to add some entry in FI to SD, then, it is always better to start from SD side by having the required pricing procedure and account determination configuration in place.

If you can explain what the Business requirement is, suitable suggestion can be given.

entry.title: Re: Producer Keeps Crashing
entry.content: Thanks Shane and BartRhoten for your input. Downloading Enable Now Launcher and reinstalling seemed to have fixed the issue.

entry.title: Behavior Change in CDS @Common.DefaultValuesFunction: Help Needed!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

I've recently encountered a puzzling issue after upgrading to CDS 8.6.1, and I'm hoping someone here might have insights or advice.

Context:

I have a CAP service that uses @Common.DefaultValuesFunction to provide default values for an action in one of my entities. This was working perfectly in CDS 7.9. The getDefaults function was executed with the Business Partner ID as the parameter, allowing me to retrieve context-specific default values.

Here is a simplified snippet of the relevant code:

 

extend service MyService with {
    @CDS.localized: false
    @(Capabilities.SortRestrictions.NonSortableProperties: [to_localData.invitationStatus.name, ])
    entity SupplierEntity as
        projection on bupa.A_BusinessPartner {
            key BusinessPartner,
            Supplier,
            BusinessPartnerFullName,
            BusinessPartnerIsBlocked as isBlocked,
            to_Supplier               : redirected to SupplierDetails,
            to_BusinessPartnerAddress : redirected to BusinessPartnerAddress,
            to_localData              : Association to one MyService.Suppliers on to_localData.ID = Supplier,
            to_customData             : Association to one SupplierCustom on to_customData.Supplier = Supplier
        }
        actions {
            @Common.DefaultValuesFunction: 'MyService.getDefaults'
            action invite @(requires: [
                'connector-advisor',
                'connector-admin'
            ])(email : getDefaultsRet:email,
               leadBuyer : getDefaultsRet:leadBuyer,
               naceCodes : getDefaultsRet:naceCodes,
               topics : getDefaultsRet:topics,
               segments : getDefaultsRet:segments);
            function getDefaults() returns getDefaultsRet;
        }
}

 

The Issue:After upgrading to CDS 8.6.1, I’ve noticed that the getDefaults function is now being executed with the position of the array (an index) instead of the Business Partner ID. This behavior was not present in CDS 7.9, where the function received the correct Business Partner ID contextually.

What I’ve Tried:

  1. Reviewing the CDS documentation: I’ve scoured the release notes and official CAP documentation to see if there were any changes to @Common.DefaultValuesFunction, but I couldn’t find anything definitive.

  2. Debugging the implementation: Adding logs confirmed that the parameter passed to getDefaults is indeed an index rather than the expected ID.

  3. Experimenting with parameter changes: I’ve tried to explicitly pass the Business Partner ID, but no success so far.

Questions for the Community:

  • Has anyone else experienced this issue with CDS 8.6.1?

  • Was there a change in how @Common.DefaultValuesFunction works in recent versions of CDS?

  • Do I need to explicitly pass the Business Partner ID through the caller logic, or is there a better way to adapt to this behavior?

Thanks and best regards
Sergen

entry.title: SAP UPS Instance to access SAP BTP Hana DB
entry.content:
Hello SAP Gurus,

I was doing some test analysis to use the UPS instance (User Provided Service), and access SAP BTP hana cloud DB. 

I created one schema(UPS_SCHEMA) and table inside it. Now i provided the details to access the DB.

Created a CAP project. Below is the MTA file, which i deployed to BTP -

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: UPS
version: 1.0.0
description: "A simple CAP project."
parameters:
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production
modules:
  - name: UPS-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      readiness-health-check-type: http
      readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
      memory: 256M
      disk-quota: 256M
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm
    provides:
      - name: srv-api # required by consumers of CAP services (e.g. approuter)
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    requires:
      - name: ups-database-external
      - name: UPS-auth

  - name: UPS-db-deployer
    type: hdb
    path: gen/db
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      memory: 256M
      disk-quota: 256M
    requires:
      - name: ups-database-external

resources:
  - name: ups-database-external
    type: org.cloudfoundry.existing-service
    parameters:
      service: User-Provided
      service-name: ups-database-external
  - name: UPS-auth
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application
      path: ./xs-security.json
      config:
        xsappname: UPS-${org}-${space}
        tenant-mode: dedicated

Once i tried to open nodejs Service link, it shows the below error -

Spoiler
502 Bad Gateway: Registered endpoint failed to handle the request.

I checked the logs of the service, which shows "out of memory".

I already have the schema and the data in the table for the particular user in hana DB.

I really stuck here. 

Looking for guidance to help me out and to understand the process.

entry.title: Error in Using Customized View in Fiori Worklist
entry.content:
I am new to SAP Fiori and working with Fiori App Extensions in Web Client for SAP Business One Hana. I have created a customized calculation view which is exposed as ODATA 4 on Service Layer and working. I can call it in Postman as GET /b1s/v1/sml.svc/TESTSLVIEW and can get the result dataset. I can get $metadata as well. But when I try to use this customized view on a Fiori Worklist, I cannot get data. It gives some errors like below.

Failed to get contexts for /b1s/v2/sml.svc/TESTSLVIEW with start index 0 and length 30 - Internal server error.
Error: Communication error: 500 Internal Server Error

entry.title: Re: Sold subscription product on invoice issued from S4ha...
entry.content:
Hi,

the mentioned fields like SoldProduct, BillingPeriodOfPerfStartDate or BillingPeriodOfPerfEndDate can be found in CDS View like I_BillingDocumentRequestItem (https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_BILLINGDOCUMENTREQUESTITEM). These attribute are also available in standard output print form template with release 2502CE!

Best regards and I'm happy to be able to help you!

Though, due to many different blogs comments and so on regarding bank communication, we have doubts on the fact that 2YM is the appropriate solution for interfacing payment files to third party tool. Can you confirm and if so could you provide attention points or tricks and tips to ensure successful implementation ?

Thank you in advance for your answer.

Thibaud

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Parameter Asking for Login Information
entry.content: The connection done via FireDAC. It is a requirement with our ERP software. I created a report in CR 2016 and I did not have this issue.

entry.title: Re: Add an accounting document to an SD sales flow
entry.content:
You're welcome! Yes, that's correct—if you want to add an accounting document after the billing process in a way that it integrates with the SD billing flow, it would typically require a custom solution (Z logic). Here's an outline of how you could approach it:

Custom Z Logic for Accounting Document Posting:

  1. Identify Trigger Point:

    • Use the SD Billing Document Save Event (e.g., during VF01 or VF02).
    • You can implement your logic using a user exit, BAdI, or an enhancement point. A commonly used user exit is USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT in the program RV60AFZZ.

  2. Develop Custom Logic for Posting Accounting Document:

    • Use a BAPI like BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST to create the additional accounting document.
    • Pass the required details (e.g., company code, GL accounts, amounts, cost center, etc.) derived from the billing document.

  3. Link the Billing Document to the Custom Accounting Entry:

    • Store the relationship between the billing document and the manually posted accounting document in a custom table. This ensures traceability and reporting consistency.

  4. Testing and Validation:

    • Test the integration to ensure that the accounting document is posted correctly and aligns with the billing document flow.
    • Verify that no duplicate postings occur.

Example Scenario:

If you want to create an additional journal entry (e.g., for a provision or adjustment) after the billing process, you could:

  • Fetch details like billing document number, customer, amount, and relevant GL accounts during the save event.
  • Call BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST to post the accounting document programmatically.
  • Link the billing document number (VBELN) with the accounting document number (BELNR) in a custom table.

Alternative Approach:

If you do not want to directly interfere with the SD flow:

  • Schedule a background job or develop a custom report to identify newly created billing documents and post the additional accounting entries in a batch process.

Final Note:

While Z-development is necessary for posting a completely separate accounting document, it is still advisable to explore standard options (e.g., adjustments using VF11 or corrections in FI) to minimize customizations.

entry.title: Re: Sending files from different folders/directories at a...
entry.content: @Ryan-Crosby Thanks Ryan! It would be very helpful if you could share those expression if you have or maybe some link for the expressions

Check the logs of your application for more information" while deploying application. If I check logs  it's showing in line 9  JWTStrategy is not constructor.

Saranya

entry.title: Convenience API Class in SAP MDG Customer
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using MDG for Customer.

There are around 10 uibb's in Create Change request page.

To open the Change request , system takes some time to open it.

Took st12 , corrected few of the points .

Observed that they have used convenience api class cl_usmd_conv_som_gov_api to take the Change request id in all the uibb feeder class get_data. Hope some performance can be fine tuned in that.

Is there a class or method where i can keep the code only once and reuse in all the uibb's get_Data till the Activation of CR.

Kindly suggest me the same.

Thanks.

KarthikS

entry.title: Re: Issue: Data Not Displaying on CDS Endpoint Despite Presence in HANA Table
entry.content: In your original post you are referring to data in HANA. So are you doing a Hybrid approach where you are running the app locally and access the HANA database in the Cloud and that's when you are not seeing an OData result?

entry.title: Re: Changing person-id-external
entry.content: Person ID Ext can be changed by using UDF upload but if you already use IAS check which field is used for IAS employeeNumber attribute or which one goes to AD for authentication. You should use user-id or employee id instead of person-id.

entry.title: PO Document sent to suppliers - modification
entry.content:
Good morning!!,

I want to modify the information in the document sent to the suppliers once the PO is approved (specifically the bottom part with the special instructions).

How can I do this?

I appreciate your assistance with this matter.

entry.title: Re: Sending files from different folders/directories at a...
entry.content: @bhargavparab I can try to provide a regular expression if I knew the file name formats, but I wouldn't be able to account for files that should not be selected potentially because that depends on other contents in said directories.

entry.title: Re: Integrating Azure Data Factory with SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management via sFTP Connector
entry.content: This is a very generic marketing update, this needs a lot more technical content in order to be validated. Kindly add the detailed flows.
